title: No-KYC Crypto Swap Wallet Trace Risk
keyQuestions: Can a no-KYC crypto swap still be traced on-chain?
What wallet traces remain visible after a swap?
Can XMR reduce wallet trace risk?
What should I avoid sharing with support or AI?
Should I use a new wallet for every no-KYC swap?
quickSteps: Map the source wallet before you open the quote, because its public history may shape the trace context of the swap.
Check what the selected network reveals, including TXID, amount, timing, token transfer, gas behavior, and reused address patterns.
Separate wallet roles so storage, source, destination, fee, and support-proof activity do not collapse into one obvious wallet trail.
Share only narrow proof when using explorers, AI chats, or support, and avoid screenshots or histories that reveal unrelated wallet data.
Proceed only when the route, source wallet, destination wallet, and privacy expectations match; stop if the public-chain facts do not fit the swap you intend to make.

issueRouting.start: The user wants to use a no-KYC crypto swap but needs to understand what wallet traces may still remain visible.

branches.if: The source wallet has a long public history or reused address
then: Do not treat no-KYC as full wallet privacy.
Map what the source wallet already reveals on the selected network.
Use wallet role separation before opening a quote.

branches.if: The route uses a transparent source asset such as BTC, ETH, or USDT
then: Check the public-chain transaction data that will remain visible.
Confirm the exact network, amount, timing, and destination wallet before sending.
Avoid publishing explorer links or screenshots that add social context.

branches.if: The swap needs support, AI help, or proof sharing
then: Share only the narrow proof needed for the exact question.
Use public TXID, chain, amount, time, pair, and visible route state only when relevant.
Never share seed phrases, private keys, wallet backups, route secret tokens, full screenshots, or unrelated wallet history.

riskNotes: No-KYC reduces identity collection, but it does not make transparent blockchain transactions invisible.
A reused wallet address can connect income, storage, swaps, support messages, and public activity into one readable pattern.
A private destination asset does not erase the public source-side transaction history.
Explorer links, AI prompts, support messages, and screenshots can add off-chain context to public-chain data.
Opening a timed quote before wallets, networks, fees, and privacy expectations are ready can create rushed and poorly checked decisions.
